objectives: Identify core building blocks across major cloud platforms
Map common workloads to appropriate core services
Explain how cloud technologies enable elasticity and managed operations
learning_path.title: Cloud Foundations: Core Services and Cloud Technologies

objectives: Design cloud architecture for a simple web application
Apply common cloud architecture patterns to meet availability and cost goals
Document architecture decisions and tradeoffs
learning_path.title: Architecture Basics: Design Cloud Architecture with Patterns

objectives: Implement least-privilege access using RBAC and policies
Protect sensitive data using KMS and secrets management
Validate access boundaries for common cloud resources
learning_path.title: Security Foundations: IAM, Policies, and Secrets

objectives: Provision a baseline environment using Infrastructure as Code
Manage configuration changes safely through versioned templates
Apply consistent naming, tagging, and environment separation
learning_path.title: Infrastructure as Code: Repeatable Environments

objectives: Select between SQL, NoSQL, and object storage for given requirements
Design data storage for durability, performance, and cost
Plan backups and recovery approaches aligned to workload needs
learning_path.title: Data Layer: Choosing and Operating Storage and Databases

objectives: Containerize an application using Docker
Define runtime configuration and image versioning practices
Assess container security and operational considerations
learning_path.title: Containers: Packaging Applications with Docker

objectives: Deploy containerized workloads to a managed Kubernetes service
Configure scaling and basic service exposure patterns
Apply cluster-level access controls and workload isolation
learning_path.title: Kubernetes: Running Workloads on Managed Clusters

objectives: Create a CI/CD pipeline that builds and deploys an application
Integrate Infrastructure as Code into deployment workflows
Implement gated releases and environment promotion
learning_path.title: CI/CD: Automated Build and Deployment Pipelines

objectives: Instrument and collect key metrics and logs for cloud workloads
Build dashboards and alerts for service health and SLO tracking
Establish actionable alerting to reduce noise
learning_path.title: Observability: Metrics, Logs, Dashboards, and Alerts

objectives: Explain scalability, consistency, and fault tolerance tradeoffs
Design failure-aware architectures and graceful degradation approaches
Evaluate system behavior under partial failures and load spikes
learning_path.title: Distributed Systems: Designing for Scale and Resilience

objectives: Assess an existing workload and identify refactoring opportunities
Plan a refactoring approach that reduces operational overhead
Validate refactoring outcomes against reliability and performance goals
learning_path.title: Cloud Refactoring: Modernizing for Managed Services and Containers

objectives: Respond to incidents in cloud using monitoring signals and runbooks
Perform triage to isolate blast radius and restore service
Produce incident reports using cloud monitoring and reporting outputs
learning_path.title: Incident Response: Detect, Triage, and Recover in Cloud

matched_occupation.description: Cloud engineers are responsible for the design, planning, management and maintenance of cloud-based systems. They develop and implement cloud-applications, handle the migration of existing on-premise applications to the cloud, and debug cloud stacks.
matched_occupation.label: cloud engineer
